briefdescription.para: Moves to specified X,Y position.
Shifts current position by +/- X,Y position, does not take display direction into consideration.
Shifts current position by +/- X,Y position, takes display direction into consideration.
Used for moving the cursor or display position.
Each character will be placed to the right of existing characters.
Each character will be placed to the left of existing characters.
Used for setting character append position.
Used to describe the mode of an auxiliary (text) display.
Turn display on.

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Turn display off.

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Set cursor enabled status on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: True to enable cursor, false to disable
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Set cursor blinking status on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Set to true to enable blinking position, false to disable
Negative errno code on other failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t direction, bool display_shift)
memberdef.name: auxdisplay_cursor_shift_set

briefdescription.para: Set cursor shift after character write and display shift.

parameterdescription.para: Sets the direction of the display when characters are written
If true, will shift the display when characters are written (which makes it look like the display is moving, not the cursor)
Negative errno code on other failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum auxdisplay_position type, int16_t x, int16_t y)
memberdef.name: auxdisplay_cursor_position_set

briefdescription.para: Set cursor (and write position) on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Type of move, absolute or offset
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Get current cursor on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Will be updated with the exact X position
Will be updated with the exact Y position
Negative errno code on other failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum auxdisplay_position type, int16_t x, int16_t y)
memberdef.name: auxdisplay_display_position_set

briefdescription.para: Set display position on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Type of move, absolute or offset
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Get current display position on an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Will be updated with the exact X position
Will be updated with the exact Y position
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Fetch capabilities (and details) of auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Will be updated with the details of the auxiliary display
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Clear display of auxiliary display and return to home position.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this does not reset the display configuration, e.g. custom characters and display mode will persist.

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the current brightness level of an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Will be updated with the current brightness
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Update the brightness level of an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the backlight level details of an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Will be updated with the current backlight level
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Update the backlight level of an auxiliary display.

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Check if an auxiliary display driver is busy.

parameterdescription.para: on success and display not busy.
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Sets a custom character in the display.

detaileddescription.para: The custom character struct must contain the pixel data for the custom character to add and valid custom character index. If successful then the character_code variable in the struct will be set to the character code that can be used with the auxdisplay_write() function to show it.

A character must be valid for a display consisting of a uint8 array of size character width by character height, values should be 0x00 for pixel off or 0xff for pixel on, if a display supports shades then values between 0x00 and 0xff may be used (display driver dependent).

parameterdescription.para: Negative errno code on other failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const uint8_t *data, uint16_t len)
memberdef.name: auxdisplay_write

briefdescription.para: Write data to auxiliary display screen at current position.

parameterdescription.para: Length of text data to write
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Send a custom command to the display (if supported by driver)

parameterdescription.para: Custom command structure (this may be extended by specific drivers)
Negative errno code on other failure.

briefdescription.para: Used for minimum and maximum brightness/backlight values if not supported.
Interfaces for auxiliary (textual/non-graphical) displays.
